heading: Protection of whistleblowers as criteria in evaluation of supervisors
label: 732 Protection of whistleblowers as criteria in evaluation of supervisors
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 732, added , , , related to treatment of whistleblower complaints, prior to repeal by , , .Pub. L. 114–223, div. A, title II, § 247(a)(1)130 Stat. 884Pub. L. 115–41, title I, § 102(a)(1)131 Stat. 865Sept. 29, 2016June 23, 2017

children.heading: .—Development and Use of Criteria Required
children.label: a .—Development and Use of Criteria Required

children.text: the Secretary shall use as a critical element in any evaluation of the performance of a supervisory employee; and

children.heading: .—Principles for Protection of Whistleblowers
children.label: b .—Principles for Protection of Whistleblowers
children.text: The criteria required by subsection (a) shall include principles for the protection of whistleblowers, such as the degree to which supervisory employees respond constructively when employees of the Department report concerns, take responsible action to resolve such concerns, and foster an environment in which employees of the Department feel comfortable reporting concerns to supervisory employees or to the appropriate authorities.

children.heading: .—Supervisory Employee and Whistleblower Defined
children.label: c .—Supervisory Employee and Whistleblower Defined
children.text: In this section, the terms “supervisory employee” and “whistleblower” have the meanings given such terms in .section 323 of this title
